{{Quote|Outworlders, I am Aragh, Lord of the Highlands, Patriarch of the S'kytri – the Windborn.|Aragh to Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SWA1" />}}
'''Aragh''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[S'kytri]] warrior and [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of the [[Highlands (Skye)|Highlands]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Skye/Legends|Skye]]. Serving as the people's [[Patriarch (Skye)|Patriarch]], he resided in the planet's capital city, the [[City of the Winged People]].

==Biography==
A blue-pigmented [[S'kytri]] of the [[Highland Clans]], Aragh served as a Guardsman and retainer to the [[Patriarch (Skye)|Patriarch]] [[Klarymére]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. At this time, the crazed [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] mutant, [[Zeta Magnus]] had brought a pathogen to Skye and unleashed it on her people, declaring himself [[Magister]] in the process. Escorting the Patriarch to a rendezvous point with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his two [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Halagad Ventor]], Aragh collected the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] from the Jedi and escorted them to the gates of the [[City of the Winged People]] high in the summits of a nearby mountain. Bringing the Jedi before the [[Supreme Council of the Highland Clans]] to discuss the future of the Tyrant which ruled over them, Aragh did Klarymére's bidding at the conclusion of the meeting and released the [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] that the S'kytri had placed under their custody when the Jedi arrived. When the Patriarch accompanied the Jedi into the [[Entyrmion]] labyrinth beneath the surface of Skye, Aragh waited dutifully back in the capital and served as steward. When Klarymére's daughter, [[Kharys]] contacted him with news of the Patriarch's death, Aragh rallied the warriors of the S'kytri people to come to her aide on the slops of [[Canaitith Mountain]] where they would do battle with the Evil Tyrant. Fighting valiantly against Magnus' thralls, Aragh witnessed the death of his friend and fellow warrior [[Herana]] and fought harder for it.<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>

[[File:9speart.jpg|thumb|left|120px]]

Sometime after Klarymére's death, Aragh succeeded him as Patriarch of the Highland Clans. When the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] was shot down by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] over the planet Skye, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] were captured by the Highland clans. Aragh brought the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] to trial before the [[Supreme Council of the Highland Clans]], and fully expected that they would be found guilty and the only thing to decide was the punishment. Since Skye had been affiliated with the Empire, and it was known that Leia Organa was a leader in the Rebellion, their fate seemed to be sealed, but councilor [[Deverén]] recognized Skywalker's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] as one that she had seen before. The council discussed the possibility that Luke might be the "one" they had been waiting for, and Aragh decided he would test Skywalker's honor. He revealed to the young Rebel that [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] had not been killed but had been captured by Majestrix [[Kharys]]. Skywalker and Organa both decided that they should invade the Majestrix's fortress, and with that resolution they passed the test and were declared to be friends of the S'kytri. Aragh led Skywalker, Organa, and members of the Highland clans to the fortress, but did not enter the battle forcing the Rebels to fight alone. After the battle was over, the Rebels had rescued their captured friends and Skywalker had killed Kharys, freeing Skye from Imperial rule.<ref name="SWA1" />
